Inadequate obturation is the most frequent cause of RCT failure. According to study done by Ingle and colleagues, 58% of treatment failures were due to incomplete obturation.

Explanation:

Chemical solvents like chloroform, xylol and Eucalyptol (plant product) softens and enables the gutta-percha to flow into small and tortuous canals.

Explanation:

Subject: Endodontics
Question: The vertical fracture of root seen with gutta percha obturation is due to:
Option A: The vertical compression of warm GP points
Explanation:
A&B' [Cohen 10th ed 305/Letchirakam V, Palamara JE, Messer HH. Finite element analysis and strain guage studies of vertical root fracture. J endod 2003:29:529- 34]
Vertical root fractures can occur both in lateral and warm vertical compaction techniques. Infact more with lateral compaction. Increased wedging forces with LC accounts for 48-84% of VRF. Cohen says that forces developed with both techniques are almost same with wedging forces of 0.65Â±0.07 kg for WVC and 0.8Â±0.1 kg for LC.

Question: Endo sealer containing polyketone is
Explanation: A' [Grossman 12th ed 284]

Explanation:

Explanation: D' [Grossman 12th ed 292]

Question: EDTA C is?

Question: Gutta-percha is radio-opaque due to presence of:
Explanation: B' [Ingle 6th ed 1020]

Subject: Endodontics
Question: The crown of an RC treated tooth is fractured near the gingival margin. The coronal end of the silver cone used in filling that canal is visible at that level. Treatment is:
Option A: To prepare post space alongside the silver cone using burs and Peso reamers
Option B: To grind away the coronal part of silver cone using round burs or end cutting burs
Option C: To remove the silver cone and re-treat the canal using a gutta-percha; and then to create the needed post space
Option D: To remove the silver cone, notch it, coat it with a freshly mixed sealer replace it in the canal and twist off the coronal segment
Explanation: C' [Grossman 12th ed 440]

Subject: Endodontics
Question: For lateral condensation of gutta-percha, the spreader should be pre-fitted to:
Explanation:
D' [Cohen 10th ed 368]
Spreader should be selected such that it is to be inserted to within 1-2 mm from the working length. When introduced into the canal with the master-cone in place, it should be within 2 mm of the working length. There appears to be a correlation between establishing a seal. and spreader penetration.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Which of the following needle is used for irrigation?
Explanation:
C' [Cohen 10th ed 246]
â¢ Larger diameter needles (23 to 24 gauge) are less efficient during irrigation as they are placed at a greater distance from the apex.
â¢ Smaller diameter needles (24 to 30 guage) can be placed closer to the apex and so they are efficient. 27 guage which has an external diameter of 0.40 mm are the most commonly used needles in endodontics.
â¢ Special 30 gauge needles like stropko Flexi tip (NiTi), Max-I-probe, Navi tips are also available.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: The main objective of obturation is:
Option A: Fill the canal and prevent apical percolation of fluids
Option B: Fill the canal and prevent discolouration of teeth
Option C: Fill the canal to give support to restoration
Explanation: A' [Grossman 12th ed 278]

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Best method of condensation
Explanation:
B>A' [Grossman 12th ed 283-84]
i) Lateral Condensation:
â¢ In this technique a primary or master gutta-percha cone is placed in the canal and the remaining space is filled by accessory or secondary cones with the help of spreaders and pluggers.
â¢ Most traditionally used. However, in contemporary endodontics it is not the best technique to achieve a three dimensional seal.
ii) Vertical condensation or warm guttapercha technique.
â¢ Introduced by Schilder
â¢ Used with step back technique of root canal preparation
â¢ After placing the guttapercha cone vertical force is applied with heated pluggers so that the guttapercha cones fill the lumen of the canal in all the three dimensions upto the apical foramen.
â¢ Risk of vertical root fracture is main disadvantage of this technique.
iii) Sectional method
â¢ This technique uses sections of gutta-percha cones to fill a section of the root canal. Void between the sections is the main disadvantage.
iv) Mcspadden compaction methods:
â¢ A rotating compacting instrument, which is similar to hedstroem file with the flutes in reverse direction, attached to a slow speed contra angle hand piece with 8000 - 10000 r.p.m, creates heat. The compactor forces the softened guttapercha apically and laterally.
v) Chemically plasticized guttapercha
â¢ Used in fine tortuous canals. Guttapercha is softened by chemical solvents like chloroform, euclapytol or xylol and is forced into fine, tortuous canal.
vi) Thermoplastic technique
â¢ Uses more heat to increase the plasticity of guttapercha and enable the operator to fill the root canal by using less pressure.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Which of the following can be used as root canal filling materials
Explanation:
D' [Grossman 12th ed 283, 285]
Cements like ZOE, Calcium hydroxide are used as obturating materials in primary teeth. Silver points are indicated in fine, tortuous canals. Guttapercha is the most commonly used obturating material. It is difficult to introduce guttapercha into a narrow root canal and it does not seal a canal apically or laterally unless it is combined with a root canal cement or sealer.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Bismuth compounds are incorporated in root canal filling materials because they are:
Explanation:
A' [Grossman 12th ed 280]
Heavy metal compounds like bismuth are added as radiopacifiers.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Most common cause of RCT failure
Explanation: C' [Cohen 10th ed 349]

Subject: Endodontics
Question: In post core which of following gutta-percha technique is used
Explanation:
C' [Grossman 11th ed 247]
Silver cones are contraindicated in filling a root canal if the tooth is to be restored with a post and core. Guttapercha is indicated as obturating material in these cases. Ideally the length of dowel or post should be 2/3rd of the root canal.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Disinfection of root canal obtained with
Explanation: D' [Grossman 12th ed 272]

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Calcium hydroxide is advocated as an inter appointment intracanal medication because of
Option A: Its ability to dissolve necrotic tissue
Option C: Its ability to stimulate hard tissue formation
Explanation: B' [Grossman 12th ed 272]

Question: Rejuvenation of gutta-percha can be changed from a to b form at
Explanation: B' [Grossman 12th ed 281]

Explanation:
C' [Grossman 12th ed 291]
Refer Q.No. 20 Mc Spadden compaction method.

Question: Fall in alkalinity of Ca(OH)2 when used as dressing starts at
Explanation: A' [Grossman 12th ed 273]

Question: The primary gutta-percha cone must fill the canal wall tightly in the:
Explanation: A' [Ingle 5th ed 601, 602/ Grossman 12th ed 283, 284]

Question: The periapical radiograph of RCT treated tooth shows wiggly apical seal of master cone is due to:

Subject: Endodontics
Question: An ideal result of endodontic treatment is deposition of which of the following at the apex:
Explanation:
B' [Ingle 5th ed 653]
Following well-executed treatment, when infection is terminated, cementoblasts and periodontal ligament slowly resurface the damaged root and even close minor foramina that no longer contain neurovascular bundles.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: All are true for root canal sealer except:
Option A: Excessive use of sealer tends to extrusion in periapical areas
Option B: Sealer mediates immune reaction in perapical region
Option C: Loss of sealer causes porosity between root filter and tooth surfaces
Option D: Sealer occupies the space between root filler and tooth surfaces
Explanation:
B' [Ingle 5th ed 368, 369]
Root canal sealer containing paraformaldehyde in any form is strictly contraindicated.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Which is not a eugenol sealer?
Explanation:
B' [Ingle 5th ed 581/ Grossman 12th ed 301,303]
EWT (Extended Working Time), Roth's sealer, Tubliseal, Wach's cement, MCS (Medicated canal sealer) and Nogenol are different modifications of ZOE cements. CRCS (Calciobiotic root canal sealer), Sealapex, Life, Apexit, Imbiseal, Vitapex are calcium hydroxide sealers. Diaket and AH-26 are examples of plastic and resin cement. Ketac-endo is GIC cement developed for endodontics. AH-26 is an epoxy resin containing a non-toxic hardener. Its radiopacity is imparted to it by bismuth oxide. It has strong adhesive properties and contracts slightly while hardening.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: MTA is used for
Explanation:
D' [Ingle 6th ed 1315]
Applications of Mineral Trioxide Aggregate:
â¢ Vital pulp therapy or Apexogenesis of any dentition.
â¢ Apexification of permanent dentition
â¢ Root end filling material
â¢ Repair of perforations
â¢ Repair of incomplete vertical root fractures
â¢ Repair of root resorptions
â¢ Obturating material

Subject: Endodontics
Question: When is an application of heated injected gutta percha potentially beneficial
Option A: when there is an open apex
Option B: when there are aberrations or irregularities of the canal
Option C: when the clinician cannot master lateral condensation
Option D: when the canals are curved and small after preparation
Explanation:
B' [Cohen 9th ed 382]
Injectable guttapercha is especially beneficial when managing canal irregularities such as fins, webs, cul-de-sacs, internal resorption, C-shaped canals, accessory or lateral canals. It is not indicated in case of open apices where there is potential for extrusion and damaging the periodontium.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Addition of synthetic resins to zinc oxide eugenol cement is said to
Explanation:
A' [Marzouk 1st ed 261]
Addition of synthetic resins (IRM, Kalzinol) increases the strength and decreases solubility.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: With obtura II system which has a high temperature gutta-percha but cools to
Explanation:
A' [Grossman 12th ed 292,293]
Guttapercha is heated to 185-200Â°C, a needle is used to deliver the guttapercha. The guttapercha cools rapidly to 70Â°C and hardens within 1 min.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Thermafil is
Option D: Is a cold gutta percha technique
Explanation:
A' [Cohen 9th ed 386/ Grossman 12th ed 297]
Thermafill contains a centre carrier (plastic / stainless steel) which is grooved along 600 of their circumference and has a coating of guttapercha. This is heated in a specific oven (Thermaprep oven) and used for obturation.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: High heat obturation technique refers to
Explanation: D' [Cohen 8th ed 345/ Grossman 12th ed 292]

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Which of the following is urethane dimethacrylate (UDMA) resin based endosealer?
Explanation:
A' [Cohen 9th ed 269]
Endorez is a visible light curable urethane dimethacrylate resin based sealer. It is hydrophilic and penetrates deep into dentinal tubules thereby reducing the microbial leakage.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Most recent form of MTA used as an intracanal irrigant?
Explanation:
C' [Cohen 9th ed 258, 322/ Grossman 12th ed 270]
MTA-D is a recently introduced irrigation solution. It is a mixture of Doxycycline, an acid (citric acid) and a detergent. In-vitro experiments indicates that MTA-D has potential for removal of smear layer and highly effective in killing E-fecalis.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: The advantage of silver point as obturating material is that it
Option A: Can be used in fine tortuous canals
Option B: Is easier to retrieve if retreatment becomes necessary
Option C: Gives a perfect seal at the apical 1/3rd of the root canal
Option D: Provides better adaptation to canal walls compared to Gutta Percha
Explanation:
A' [Grossman 12th ed 281]
As silver cone is stiffer than gutta-percha, it can be easily inserted into a fine tortuous canal.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Vertical compaction of warm gutta-percha technique was given by?
Explanation:
C' [Grossman 11th ed 287]
The warm vertical condensation or warm gutta-percha technique of filling root canals was introduced by Schilder with the objective of filling lateral and accessory canals along with the main canal.

Question: Extrusion of sealer from root apex will cause
Explanation: A' [Cohen 10th ed 425]

Question: Master cone is
Option A: Fully snug fit with accurate working length

Question: AH plus & AH 26 are

Question: True about vertical compaction technique is:

Subject: Endodontics
Question: All are true regarding ideal properties of root canal materials except:
Explanation:
A' [Grossman 12th ed 279]
Ideal requirements of a filling material are:
â¢ Easy to introduce and easy to remove from the root canal
â¢ Seal canal laterally as well as apically
â¢ Impervious to moisture
â¢ Should not shrink after insertion and should expand slightly after placement
â¢ Non-resorbable for an indefinite period of time
â¢ Should be bactericidal
â¢ Should not stain the tooth
â¢ Should not stain irritate periapex
â¢ Should be sterile or sterilizable
â¢ Preferably semisolid upon insertion and solid afterward
â¢ Radiopaque
â¢ Able to set in a reasonable period of time

Question: Silver cone used as a root canal filling material is indicated in
Option A: Narrow curved canals in which enlargement beyond a No.20 /25 instrument is unadvisable.
Option C: Surgical cases in which root resection is anticipated
Option D: Incomplete large or irregular canals of a young patient.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: The Lorothidol added in non-eugenol paste has the function of
Explanation:
B' [Shanthipriya reddy Periodontics 2nd ed 296]
2 Types of periodontal pacts are available. They are ZOE pack and non-eugenol pack.
â¢ Non-eugenol pack is based on the reaction between a metallic oxide and fatty acids. It is supplied in 2 tubes.
Composition of non-eugenol pack:
â¢ One tube contains zinc oxide, oil for plasticity, a gum for cohesiveness and lorothiodol a fungicide.
â¢ The other tube contains liquid coconut fatty acids thickened with rosin and chlorothymol. a bacteriostatic agent.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Advocated apical seal for dowel core is
Explanation:
B' [Grossman 12th ed 447]
The minimum amount of GP that should be left during post space preparation for adequate apical seal is 4-5 mm.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: The term TUG BACK is used to indicate
Option A: Good fit of accessory gutta-percha point at the apical third
Option B: Good fit of master gutta-percha point at the apical third
Option C: Good fit of accessory cone in the main body of the canal
Option D: Good fit of the initial file at the apical third
Explanation: B' [Grossman 12th ed 283]

Subject: Endodontics
Question: The most common consequence of bleaching non vital teeth is
Explanation:
B' [Grossman 12th ed 349/ Sturdevant 4th ed 608]
Cervical resorption is a common consequence of bleaching of non-vital teeth whereas apical periodontitis is a common sequel of bleaching of vital teeth. The possibility of cervical resorption can be reduced by sealing the root canal orifice with cavit to prevent percolation of bleaching agent or by keeping a paste of calcium hydroxide and sterile water in the pulp chamber.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Superoxol is
Option B: Combination of H2O2 + sodium per borate
Explanation:
A' [Ingle 6th ed 1389]
Superoxol is a 30% solution of hydrogen peroxide by weight and 100% by volume in pure distilled water. Superoxol is mixed with sodium perborate into a paste and is used in "walking bleach" procedure.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Home bleach is also called as
Explanation:
A' [Sturdevant 4th ed 609/ Grossman 12th ed 349]
I. Nonvital Bleaching Techniques.
â¢ Thermo catalytic or in office technique
â¢ Walking bleach or out of the office or home technique.
II. Vital Bleaching Techniques.
â¢ Power bleach or in office technique.
â¢ Night guard or dentist prescribed home technique.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: In the walking bleach technique:
Option B: It requires the patient to report in 24 hours
Option C: Can be done with 35 percent hydrogen peroxide
Option D: It uses a mixture of sodium perborate and hydrogen peroxide
Explanation:
D' [Grossman 12th ed 349, Sturdevant 4th ed 609]
Option 'B' is in office vital bleach technique. Option 'D' is home vital bleach technique. Option 'A' walking bleach, the bleach material is kept in pulp chamber for up to 1 week and is known as at home nonvital bleach technique.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: When undertaking bleaching of vital teeth of the following are done except:
Explanation: C' [Grossman 12th ed 353. Sturdevant 4th ed 610]

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Tooth discoloration is due to:
Explanation:
C' [Grossman 12th ed 345]
Pulpal death is most common cause of tooth discoloration. There is breakdown of haemoglobin into various pigments like hemin, hematin, hemosiderin, hematoporphyrin. Hydrogen sulfide produced by bacteria combines with these pigments and darkens the tooth.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Carbamide solution used for bleaching degrades into
Explanation:
C' [Cohen 8th ed 755/ Grossman 12th ed 348]
Carbamide peroxide degrades into 3% hydrogen peroxide (active ingredient) and 7% urea.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Vital bleaching causes
Explanation: D' [Grossman 12th ed 349]

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Night guard bleaching refers to:
Explanation:
B' [Grossman 12th ed 353]
Dentist prescribed home bleaching methods of vital teeth or night guard bleaching is treatment of choice. Ans 'A' is for microabrasion, a method used to remove surface stains or defects. Ans 'C' is for non vital teeth.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: The bleaching material used for night guard vital tooth bleaching is
Explanation: D' [Ingle 6th ed 1394]

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Discoloration of the teeth results from
Explanation: D' [Grossman 12th ed 344]

Subject: Endodontics
Question: In case of bleaching technique to remove fluorosis stains
Explanation:
D' [Grossman 12th ed 358]
Composition of Mc-Inns solution used for removal of endemic flourosis stains is
| Component | Volume | Function |
|---|---|---|
| 1 part anesthetic ether | 0.2 ml | Removal of surface debris |
| 5 parts of HCL (36%) | 1ml | Etching of enamel |
| 5 parts of H202 (30%) | 1 ml | Bleaching of enamel |

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Which of the following is not responsible for endogenous staining of teeth during development?
Explanation: D' [Grossman 12th ed 344]

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Discoloration of tooth in tetracycline therapy is because of formation of?
Explanation:
A' [Text book of oral medicine by Ghom 3rd ed 119]
Tetracycline causes discoloration during formation period. It reacts with calcium to form calcium orthophosphate complex. The minimum amount required to produce discoloration is 21 mg/kg/body weight.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: The "in office" non vital bleaching technique is
Explanation: C' [Cohen 8th ed 755/ Grossman 12th ed 348]

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Following intracoronal bleaching immediate composite restoration required, what has to be done:
Explanation:
A' [Grossman 12th ed 352]
Composites should be delayed for 2-3 weeks after bleaching, to allow for dissipation of residual peroxides that would interfere with the bonding agents. Catalase treatment at the final visit may enhance removal of residual peroxides but it is still under investigation. Sodium Ascorbate can also be used. It is a buffered form of vitamin C that consists of 90% Ascorbic acid bound to 10% sodium, a powerful antioxidant useful in eliminating residual oxygen following bleaching.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: A twenty year old female patient comes to the clinic with discoloration of the upper right central incisor. This tooth was intact and otherwise asymptomatic. The most likely cause for the discoloration in this patient would be
Explanation:
A' [Grossman 12th ed 345]
In this case, the discoloration is present in a single tooth. So, pulp necrosis is the most likely cause.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: The treatment most often employed for managing discoloration in a non-vital teeth is
Explanation: B' [Q.No.3 and 4]

Subject: Endodontics
Question: The common sequel following bleaching non vital teeth are:
Explanation: C' [Explanation of Q.No.1]

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Bleaching is indicated in which of the following clinical situations
Explanation:
A' [Grossman 12th ed 346]
Bleaching agents like HâOâ can penetrate dentin and release oxygen that breaks down the double bond of organic and inorganic compounds inside the tubule. Options B, C and D are contraindications of bleaching.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Glass ionomer cement is used as a barrier over gutta-percha filing before bleaching an endodontically treated discolored tooth.
Option A: To prevent bleaching agent from dissolving the gutta-percha.
Option B: To prevent percolation of the bleaching agent into the apical area
Option C: To prevent contamination of bleaching agent
Option D: To prevent discoloration of tooth from obturating material.
Explanation:
B' [Grossman 12th ed 349]
â¢ Quality of seal in the apical part as well coronal part of the root canal system is important to prevent percolation of fluids. In non-vital bleaching, Cavit or GIC or MTA is placed as a barrier material over the root canal filling material.
â¢ The level of barrier should be 1mm incisal to CEJ.
â¢ This prevents percolation of bleaching agent into the root canal filling and nearby into the periapex.
â¢ This also confines the bleaching agent to the crown thereby preventing cervical root resorption.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Till what age tetracycline should not be given to prevent discoloration
Explanation:
B' [Check KDT 6th ed 714]
Tetracycline when given from mid pregnancy to 5 months of extrauterine life, the deciduous teeth are affected with discoloration. When given between 3 months to 6 years of age, they affect the crowns of permanent anterior dentition. They should be avoided from 4 months in utero to 8 years.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Minimum dosage of tetracycline which will show tooth discoloration is
Explanation:
B' [Check Journal of Paediatric dentistry 1987;9(3)- 245-46]
Many clinical studies revealed that average dose consumption of 20-35 mg/kg body weight / day during tooth formation results in discoloration. Doses > 35 mg/ kg/day results in both discoloration and hypoplasia of enamel.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Microbrasion is a procedure in clinical orthodontics performed to
Explanation:
D' [Graber 4th ed 621]
After the braces are removed, the first step is treatment of white spot lesions by allowing natural remineralization. The second step is external bleaching to help camouflage white spots. A third step for patients with severe white spot is acid microabrasion to eliminate the external layer of the lesion, followed by application of CPP-ACP (recaldent) paste. When demineralizing capacity of the oral fluids is exhausted and white spots are established microabrasion is the optimal way to remove superficial enamel opacities. By this one can eliminate enamel stains with minimal enamel loss. This can be achieved with a custom made gel consisting of 18% HCl, pumice and glycerine. This is applied for 5 minutes with an electric tooth brush and then rinsed. This process is repeated 2 to 3 times a month.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: A 12-year-old patient who experienced dental trauma a year ago. Upon examination, it's discovered that the affected tooth presents with a calcified canal, yellow discoloration, and a periapical index of 2. Given this clinical presentation, what treatment plan would be most appropriate?
Explanation:
B' [Check Explanation Below/ Check Ques No 15 RECENT PAPERS 2020-21 ENDODONTICS]
â¢ All the clinical presentations of the affected tooth i.e. a calcified canal, yellow discoloration, and a periapical index score of 2 indicate that a tooth has become non vital and hence, endodontic treatment is needed.
â¢ In Periapical index, a score of 2 indicates small changes in bone structures, and is considered as healthy. For managing yellow discoloration, root canal treatment followed by non-vital bleaching is the best treatment option considering the age of the patient (crown not advised). Veneer will be overprescribed option for mild stains.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: All of the following incisions are recognized, standard approaches for an apicoectomy EXCEPT.
Option A: Cervical margin of teeth with buccal extensions
Explanation: B' [Grossman 12th ed 401-02]

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Which is true about mucogingival flap designs
Option A: Flaps should be wider at the base
Option B: Flaps should be narrower at the base
Option C: Flap margins should not rest on the bone
Explanation:
A' [Grossman 12th ed 401]
Cardinal principles of flap design:
â¢ The base of the flap should be wider than the free end to ensure adequate circulation into the flap.
â¢ The sutured flap margins should rest on solid cortical bone plate.
â¢ Incision should be made with a firm, continuous stroke, perpendicular to cortical bone. Short intermittent incisions result in tissue tags and ragged margins.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Which surgical procedure is indicated after the endodontic treatment is completed of a mandibular molar with periodontal and carious involvement of the bifurcation:
Explanation: A' [Grossman 12th ed 434]

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Apicoectomy is indicated in which one of the following cases?
Option A: Persistent positive results of root canal culture for microorganisms
Option B: Incomplete obturation in apical part of root canal with persistent infection
Option C: Fracture of apical one-third area of root in a vital asymptomatic tooth
Option D: Presence of a minor pulp stone in pulp chamber
Explanation: B' [Grossman 12th ed 391]

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Which of the following are complications of Periapical surgery
Explanation:
D' [Grossman 12th ed 410]
Paraesthesia is more common in mandible, especially in the premolar and molar region (due to accidental penetration of mandibular canal). Stitch abscess may develop due to local laceration of tissue or accumulation of debris at site of suturing or tying of knot in the line of incision. Ecchymosis results in skin discoloration from extravasation and breakdown of blood adjacent to surgical area

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Endodontic surgery should be avoided in:
Explanation:
D' [Grossman 12th ed 392]
Because of inaccessibility and difficulty to gain access to surgical site through dense layers of bone, endodontic surgery should be avoided on lingual surfaces of molars or external oblique ridge of mandible

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Draining abscess by cutting a window in the bone is
Explanation:
B' [Grossman 12th ed 415/Ingle 6th ed 1234]
Trephination is a type of artificial fistulation in which, the cortical bone is perforated to release the build up pressure and exudate around root apex to release pain.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: RCT treated roots with well filled post and core, has a periapical radiolucency
Option A: Apicoectomy and retrograde filling with silver
Explanation: A' [Grossman 12th ed 391]

Subject: Endodontics
Question: In Periapical surgery, which of the following flap designs limit access to the operative site and often heals with scar formation
Explanation:
C' [Grossman 12th ed 401]
Inaccessibility, excessive hemorrhage, delayed healing, and scarring are the disadvantages of semilunar incision. It is used when no underlying periodontal problems are present i.e when there is no extensive bone loss. Inspite of its disadvantages, it is most oftenly used in routine apical surgery because of high healing potential and minimal bone damage associated with semilunar flap.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: The prognosis of the broken instrument in root canal is good when
Option B: Instrument is lodged in the bone beyond the apical area
Option C: Instrument obliterates apical third of the canal
Explanation:
C' [Grossman 12th ed 487]
In such cases, the instrument is removed by apicoectomy followed by retrograde filling

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Apicoectomy is contraindicated in
Explanation: A' [Grossman 12th ed 392]

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Replacement resorption is characterized by:
Explanation: D' [Grossman 12th ed 387]

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Apical surgery is least indicated in
Explanation:
D' [Grossman 12th ed 392]
Apical surgery is done with great caution on mandibular bicuspids because of their proximity to the mental foramen.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Most of the failures of replanted tooth are due to
Explanation:
C' [Grossman 12th ed 442]
External inflammatory resorption is the most frequent failure to replantation. Three differnet types of resorption have been identified: surface, inflammatory, and replacement (ankylotic resorption). Replacement resorption refers to resorption of the root surface and its substitution by bone, resulting in ankylosis.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Which of the following directly affects the prognosis of an avulsed tooth:
Option A: Length of time the tooth was out of the mouth
Option B: Condition of the dental socket when the tooth was replanted
Option C: Whether necrotic cementum was removed from the root
Option D: Whether the pulp was extirpated to prevent Periapical inflammation
Explanation:
A' [Grossman 12th ed 380]
Though 'B', 'C', 'D' also influences the prognosis of avulsed tooth, extra oral time is the most critical factor affecting prognosis. Prolonged extraoral time causes dehydration and death of root surface cells and impairment of viability of periodontal ligament.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: When performing endodontic surgery on anterior crowned teeth, the ideal surgical flap design is a
Explanation:
C' [Grossman 12th ed 403]
The submarginal scalloped rectangular flap is also called as Leubke- Ochsenbein flap. This flap is ideal for crowned teeth when crown margins after surgery are an esthetic concern.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: The flap design ideal for root end surgical procedures in upper anterior teeth with ceramic crowns is the
Explanation: D' [Grossman 12th ed 403]

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Root-end preparation during microsurgical endodontics is done by ultrasonic:
Explanation:
C' [Ingle 7th ed 818]
The use of ultrasonic tips is currently considered the standard of practice in surgical endodontics. They prepare deeper and cleaner preparations and address anatomical difficulties. But they can cause root crack formation. Lower power settings reduce the number of micro-fractures. They should always be used with water coolant. They normally have irrigation port near the tip or along the shaft.
